2605.26683.title: An In-Vitro Study on Cross-Lingual Generalization in Language Models
2605.26683.paper_summary: Cross-lingual transfer in language models is difficult to study in natural corpora because lexical overlap, morphology, data imbalance, and tokenization are entangled. We introduce an in-vitro framework with two procedurally generated languages that share the same ontology, typed grammar, and compositional structure, but differ in surface realization. This lets us independently vary lexical distance, minority-language proportion, tokenizer training regime, and vocabulary size, while evaluating transfer on a masked minority-language condition whose lexical forms are never observed during training. Across 700 controlled runs, we find that transfer is governed less by tokenizer balance or raw lexical similarity than by whether tokenization preserves reusable cross-lingual substructure. Smaller vocabularies often improve masked transfer by keeping words decomposable into shared fragments, whereas larger vocabularies can turn forms into language-specific atoms. We further show that transfer emerges as a staged process: grammatical and type-level competence precede masked lexical generalization. Finally, we attempt to explain this mechanism through tokenizer bridges and show that bridge strength correlates strongly with masked reachability.
2605.26683.paper_summary_zh: 跨語言轉移在語言模型中難以在自然語料中研究，因為詞彙重疊、形態學、數據不平衡和標記化交織在一起。<lb/>我們引入了一個體外框架，使用兩種程序生成的語言，這兩種語言共享相同的本體、類型語法和組合結構，但在表面實現上有所不同。<lb/>這使我們能夠獨立變化詞彙距離、少數語言比例、標記器訓練方案和詞彙大小，同時在一個掩蔽的少數語言條件下評估轉移，該條件下的詞彙形式在訓練期間從未被觀察到。<lb/>在700次受控實驗中，我們發現轉移的主導因素不在於標記器的平衡或原始詞彙相似性，而在於標記化是否保留可重用的跨語言子結構。<lb/>較小的詞彙通常通過保持單詞可分解為共享片段來改善掩蔽轉移，而較大的詞彙則可能將形式轉變為語言特定的原子。<lb/>我們進一步顯示轉移作為一個分階段的過程出現：語法和類型層次的能力先於掩蔽的詞彙概括。<lb/>最後，我們試圖通過標記器橋來解釋這一機制，並顯示橋的強度與掩蔽可達性之間存在強相關性。
2605.26683.author: Adrian Cosma et.al.
